expectations.text: Reads or references scene-briefs.csv as the primary data source
expectations.evidence: Analyzes scene-briefs.csv in detail, quoting key_actions, key_dialogue, emotional arcs

expectations.text: Mentions abstract language, abstract indicators, or thematic vs concrete language
expectations.evidence: Does not use hone's abstract/concrete detection framework. Discusses 'over-specification' and 'prescriptive' briefs instead — a different (and arguably insightful) framing, but not the hone concept

expectations.text: Names at least one specific scene ID from the project data
expectations.evidence: Names all scenes with detailed per-scene analysis

expectations.text: Recommends the briefs domain or concretization as the action
expectations.evidence: Recommends manual revision of briefs as Option A, mentions storyforge-hone as Option B but frames it as secondary. Does not use 'briefs domain' or 'concretization' terminology

expectations.text: Offers script delegation with Option A (run here) and Option B (run yourself) pattern
expectations.evidence: Provides a script command but not the formal Option A/B delegation pattern from the skill. Frames it as 'two paths' with manual revision as the primary option
